speaker: patient
text: Hi, Doctor. I've been feeling really nauseous since yesterday morning.

speaker: doctor
text: Thanks for coming in. Let’s start with a quick assessment. Are you experiencing any other symptoms like fever, dizziness, or chest discomfort?

speaker: doctor
text: Your diastolic blood pressure is 108 mmHg — that’s elevated.

speaker: doctor
text: Your temperature is 38.8 °C — you do have a fever.

speaker: doctor
text: You're currently on Lisinopril 20mg once daily — correct?

speaker: doctor
text: Your heart rate is quite high at 126 beats per minute.

speaker: patient
text: Yes, I’ve been on Lisinopril for about six months. But lately, my headaches have gotten worse, and I feel shaky sometimes.

speaker: doctor
text: That’s important — the combination of hypertension, tachycardia, and fever suggests something more acute may be going on. Any recent infections or exposure to sick contacts?

speaker: patient
text: My daughter had the flu last week, and I’ve had a sore throat since Tuesday.

speaker: doctor
text: Let’s check your throat and lungs. Also, we’ll need bloodwork and possibly a urinalysis to rule out sepsis or UTI.

speaker: patient
text: Okay. My nausea got worse this morning — I couldn’t keep breakfast down.

speaker: doctor
text: Given your persistent tachycardia and rising fever, we need to adjust your antihypertensive therapy — Lisinopril alone isn’t controlling your hemodynamics well today.

speaker: doctor
text: I’m going to switch you to Amlodipine 10mg once daily instead — it’s better tolerated in the setting of acute illness and won’t affect your renal perfusion as much.

speaker: doctor
text: Just rechecked — your heart rate is now 113 bpm, still elevated but slightly improved.

speaker: doctor
text: Your diastolic pressure has crept up to 110 mmHg since the first reading.

speaker: doctor
text: And your temperature has risen further — now 39.4 °C. We’ll start empiric antibiotics after cultures.

speaker: patient
text: That’s concerning… I didn’t realize it was getting worse so fast.

speaker: doctor
text: We’ll monitor you closely. I’d also like to admit you for IV fluids and observation — your nausea and fever suggest possible dehydration and systemic inflammation.

speaker: patient
text: Understood. Should I stop the Lisinopril myself before starting the Amlodipine?

speaker: patient
text: Actually, when I checked my pulse just now on my watch, it’s back up to 126 bpm — same as that reading earlier.
